hypotheses: Hypothesis 1: People who perceive greater realistic threats from COVID-19 will comply more with public health measures.
Hypothesis 2: People who perceive greater symbolic threats from COVID-19 will comply less with public health measures.
claim.hypotheses_location: The hypotheses are suggested in the abstract and introduction sections.
claim.statement: This study aims to determine the psychological impact of different types of threats posed by COVID-19 on compliance with public health guidelines and psychological well-being.

data.wave_or_subset: Subset from March 19-20, 2020 and pre-COVID subset from October 2019
data.sample_size: 399 participants initially recruited, reduced to 346 after exclusions.
data.notes: The study used a longitudinal and cross-sectional design with both pre- and post-COVID datasets for analysis.

method.description: The study utilized surveys and assessments to measure perceived COVID-19 threats and their effects on compliance with public health measures and psychological well-being.
steps: Administer the Integrated COVID-19 Threat Scale.
Conduct Principal Component Analysis for factor assessment.
Perform Confirmatory Factor Analysis to validate factor structure.
Analyze data using Structural Equation Modeling to assess relationships between threat perceptions and outcomes.
method.models: Principal Component Analysis, Confirmatory Factor Analysis, Structural Equation Modeling
method.outcome_variable: Compliance with public health measures, psychological well-being
method.independent_variables: Perceived realistic and symbolic COVID-19 threats
method.control_variables: Prior anxiety levels, demographic factors (if available)

results.summary: The study found that realistic threat perception from COVID-19 was associated with higher compliance with public health measures, whereas symbolic threats decreased compliance but increased behaviors affirming social identity.

numerical_results.effect_size: Realistic threat correlation, b=1.23, p<.001; Symbolic threat correlation, b=-0.67, p<.001
numerical_results.direction: Realistic threat: positive; Symbolic threat: negative

metadata.original_paper_title: An Integrative Scale of Realistic and Symbolic Threats: Exploring the Psychological Impacts of COVID-19 on Public Health Compliance
